function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
Following the xAI and X Holdings mergers, what related-party relationships exist within the consolidated structure, and where does the filing discuss pre-merger intercompany activity?

steps.reasoning_content:
The user is asking about related-party relationships within the consolidated structure after the xAI and X Holdings mergers, and where the filing discusses pre-merger intercompany activity. I need to retrieve this information from the SpaceX S-1 filing.

Let me first parse the filing and then retrieve relevant sections about related-party relationships and intercompany activity.

results.content: SUCCESS: Loaded portable assets for key: spaceX_s1

steps.reasoning_content: Good, the filing is loaded. Now let me retrieve information about related-party relationships and intercompany activity.

arguments.prompt: In {{spaceX_s1}}, find all discussions of related-party transactions, related-party relationships, and related-party disclosures within the consolidated entity including xAI and X Holdings. Look for sections discussing related parties, affiliated transactions, or intercompany relationships.

results.content:
Based on the provided document excerpts, here is a comprehensive summary of all discussions regarding **related-party transactions, relationships, and disclosures** within the consolidated entity (SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings).

### **I. Criteria & Scope of Disclosed Transactions**
*   **Effective Date:** Relationships and transactions since January 1, 2023.
*   **Threshold:** Transactions where the amount involved exceeded or will exceed **$120,000**.
*   **Parties Involved:** Directors, executive officers, holders of more than 5% of capital stock, their affiliates, and immediate family members.
*   **Scope Expansion:** Due to the **common-control mergers**, disclosures include ongoing agreements originally entered into by xAI or X Holdings prior to their mergers with SpaceX.

---

### **II. Key Related-Party Relationships**
1.  **Elon Musk:** Founder, CEO, CTO, Chairman, and principal shareholder of SpaceX. Also, CEO, Technoking, director, and ~20% shareholder of Tesla.
2.  **Antonio J. Gracias:** Board member of SpaceX. Also founder, CEO, and Chief Investment Officer of **Valor Equity Partners** and its affiliates.
3.  **Gwynne Shotwell & Bret Johnsen:** President/COO and CFO, respectively, who co-own a personal aircraft.

---

### **III. Significant Related-Party Transactions & Agreements**

#### **A. Transactions with Elon Musk & Affiliated Entities**
*   **Security Services:** SpaceX incurred expenses for security services provided by a Musk-owned company. Expenses: **$2M (2023), $3M (2024), $4M (2025), $1M (Jan-Feb 2026).**
*   **Real Estate Leases:**
    *   Lease from **Musk Industries LLC** (owned by Musk): Payments by xAI: **$0.5M (2024), $2M (2025), $0.2M (Jan-Feb 2026).**
    *   Lease from **The Boring Company** (Musk-affiliated): Payments by X: **$0.1M (2024), $1M (2025), $0.1M (Jan-Feb 2026).**
*   **Construction Services:** SpaceX incurred **$1M in 2025** for tunnel construction by The Boring Company.
*   **Aircraft Transactions:**
    *   Maintenance of a SpaceX-owned aircraft by **Craft Aviation Services, LLC** (Musk affiliate): **$1M (2023), $1M (2024), $3M (2025), $1M (Jan-Feb 2026).**
    *   SpaceX incurred expenses when Musk used his personal aircraft for business: **$0.1M (2023), $3M (2024), $2M (2025), $0.2M (Jan-Feb 2026).**
*   **Stock Purchases:** In 2025, Musk purchased **$1,421 million** of SpaceX common stock.

#### **B. Transactions with Antonio J. Gracias & Valor Equity Partners**
*   **Major Equipment Leases (AI Infrastructure Hardware):**
    *   Three separate lease agreements with aggregate cash payments over the life of each lease of **$6,986M, $6,633M, and $6,587M**.
    *   **Total Payments:** **$885M (2025), $857M (Jan-Feb 2026).**
    *   **Accounting Treatment:** Deemed **failed sale-leaseback transactions**, recorded as debt.
    *   **Balance Sheet Impact (Mar 31, 2026):** Debt of **$1,121M (current)** and **$7,920M (non-current)**.
    *   **Interest Expense (Q1 2026):** **$186M**.
*   **API Service Payments:** X received payments from Valor for API services: **$1M (2024), $1M (2025), $0.1M (Jan-Feb 2026).**

#### **C. Transactions with Tesla, Inc.**
*   **Purchases of Megapack Energy Storage & Vehicles:**
    *   **Megapacks:** **$191M (2024), $506M (2025), $34M (Q1 2026).**
    *   **Cybertrucks:** **$131M (2025).**
*   **Tesla's Investment in xAI:** Tesla invested **$2,000M** in xAI via Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock.
    *   **Post-Merger Conversion:** This investment converted into the right to acquire **3.8 million shares of SpaceX Class A Common Stock** (pre-split), issued in March 2026.

#### **D. Aircraft Use by Other Directors & Officers**
*   Gwynne Shotwell and Bret Johnsen occasionally used their co-owned aircraft for SpaceX business when company aircraft were unavailable, subject to reimbursement.

---

### **IV. Accounting & Presentation of Related-Party Mergers**
*   **Common Control Mergers:** The acquisitions of xAI and X by SpaceX were accounted for as reorganizations under **common control** (due to Elon Musk's controlling interest).
*   **Retrospective Adjustment:** Financial statements for all periods presented have been **retrospectively recast** to combine the net assets and results of SpaceX, xAI, and X at historical carrying amounts.
*   **Elimination of Intercompany Transactions:** All historical related-party transactions **between the merged entities** have been eliminated in consolidation.
*   **Change in Reporting Entity:** This retrospective combination constitutes a change in reporting entity.
*   **2026 Stock Split:** A five-for-one forward stock split effective May 4, 2026, has been retroactively applied to all share and per-share data.

---

### **V. Legal Proceedings Involving Related Parties**
*   **NAACP vs. X.AI Corp & MZX Tech, LLC:** Lawsuit alleging Clean Air Act violations related to mobile gas turbines powering the **COLOSSUS II** data center (affiliated with xAI). A preliminary injunction motion has been filed.

---

### **VI. Summary of Financial Impact**
*   **Major Balance Sheet Items:**
    *   **Debt to Valor:** ~**$9.04 billion** (current + non-current) as of Mar 31, 2026.
    *   **Litigation Accrual:** **$399 million** recorded as of Mar 31, 2026.
*   **Significant Cash Flows:**
    *   Payments to Valor for leases: **$1.74 billion** in the 14-month period ending Feb 28, 2026.
    *   Payments to Tesla for products: Over **$800 million** in 2024-2026 (YTD).
    *   Cash paid in xAI Merger: **$2.947 billion**.

**Note:** All data is sourced from the provided excerpts and reflects the combined entity following the common-control mergers.

steps.reasoning_content: Good, I have a comprehensive overview. Let me also look for specific sections about pre-merger intercompany activity and where exactly the filing discusses these topics.

arguments.prompt: In {{spaceX_s1}}, find discussions of pre-merger intercompany activity between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings before the mergers. Look for any sections discussing intercompany transactions that were eliminated in consolidation, pre-merger balances between the entities, and the specific filing locations (e.g., Note numbers) where this disclosure appears.

results.content:
Based on the provided excerpts from SpaceX's financial statements and prospectus, here is a summary of the discussions regarding pre-merger intercompany activity between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings.

### 1. Accounting Treatment & General Disclosure of Elimination
The financial statements repeatedly explain that because the mergers were accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control (all controlled by Elon Musk), the consolidated financial statements were prepared through a **retrospective combination** of the entities' historical net assets.

**Key points from the preamble and notes:**
*   **Historical Carrying Amounts:** The net assets of xAI and X were combined at their historical carrying amounts, meaning no new goodwill or intangible assets were recognized.
*   **Elimination of Transactions:** A critical part of this retrospective consolidation was the elimination of all historical transactions between the entities. The statements explicitly state: **"all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation"** and **"All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation."**
*   **Change in Reporting Entity:** This presentation is considered a change in reporting entity, and as a result, separate financial statements for xAI and X are not provided.

### 2. Specific Pre-Merger Intercompany Transactions (Detailed Disclosures)
The most detailed disclosures of the specific pre-merger intercompany activity are found in the **"Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions"** section. This section itemizes transactions that occurred between the entities (and their affiliates) prior to the mergers, which were subsequently eliminated upon consolidation.

**Disclosed transaction types include:**

*   **Commercial & Licensing Agreements:**
    *   **SpaceX with Tesla:** Obtained goods and services under commercial agreements. (Amounts cited for 2023, 2024, 2025, and early 2026).
    *   **xAI with Tesla:** Obtained goods and services under commercial agreements. xAI also recognized revenue from Tesla. (Amounts cited for 2024, 2025, and early 2026).
    *   **X (via X Holdings) with Tesla:** Tesla purchased advertising on the X platform. (Amounts cited for 2024, 2025, and early 2026).

*   **Asset Usage & Leases:**
    *   **Aircraft Usage:** SpaceX charged Tesla for the use of aircraft. (Amounts cited for 2023, 2024, 2025, and early 2026).
    *   **Office Space Leases:** X leased office space from a subsidiary of The Boring Company (affiliated with Musk). xAI leased property from Musk Industries LLC. (Amounts cited for 2024, 2025, and early 2026).
    *   **Construction Services:** SpaceX incurred expenses for tunnel construction by The Boring Company. (Amount cited for 2025).

*   **Security Services:** SpaceX incurred expenses for security services provided by a company owned by Elon Musk. (Amounts cited for 2023, 2024, 2025, and early 2026).

*   **Equipment Leases:** Certain subsidiaries of xAI entered into equipment lease and sublease agreements with Valor Equity Partners (affiliated with board member Antonio J. Gracias).

### 3. Filing Locations for This Disclosure
Based on the provided text, the key locations where these topics are discussed are:

1.  **Preamble / Nature of Business (Note 1):** This is where the general accounting policy for the mergers is explained, including the retrospective combination and the principle of eliminating intercompany transactions. (Referenced in excerpts titled *"This preamble section details the 2025 mergers..."* and *"This chunk from the financial statement notes explains the accounting..."*).
2.  **Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions:** This is the primary section for the **detailed enumeration** of the specific intercompany transactions that occurred pre-merger. It lists the parties, types of transactions, and dollar amounts for each year. (Referenced in excerpts titled *"This chunk belongs to the 'Related Party Transactions' section..."* and *"This chunk details related-party transactions..."*).
3.  **Note 2 - Summary of Significant Accounting Policies:** This note reaffirms the broad principle that **"All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation."**

**In summary:** The general policy of eliminating pre-merger intercompany activity is outlined in the accounting notes (primarily Note 1 and Note 2), while the detailed inventory of those specific transactions—commercial agreements, leases, services, and their monetary values—is extensively disclosed in the **"Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions"** section of the filing.

steps.reasoning_content: Now let me get more specific details about the intercompany balances that were eliminated and any specific amounts mentioned for pre-merger intercompany activity.

arguments.prompt: In {{spaceX_s1}}, find the specific amounts of intercompany balances between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings that were eliminated in consolidation. Look for any balance sheet line items showing intercompany receivables, payables, or equity eliminations. Also look for any discussion of the $2.947 billion cash paid in the xAI merger and any other specific intercompany balance amounts.

results.content:
Based on the excerpts from SpaceX's financial statements and prospectus, here are the specific details regarding the intercompany balances and the cash paid in the xAI merger:

### 1. Specific Intercompany Balances Eliminated in Consolidation
The documents do not list a detailed, line-by-line breakdown of every intercompany receivable and payable that was eliminated. Instead, they provide the overarching accounting principle and the net effect.

*   **Accounting Treatment:** The mergers were accounted for as **reorganizations of entities under common control**. This required the retrospective combination of the net assets of SpaceX, xAI, and X at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented.
*   **Elimination of Intercompany Transactions:** A core feature of this accounting is the **complete elimination of all historical intercompany balances and transactions**. The text explicitly states:
    *   "All historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."
    *   "All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation." (from Note 2 on Significant Accounting Policies).
*   **Effect on Financial Statements:** Because the financial statements are presented as if the companies had always been consolidated, there is no separate, highlighted line item for "eliminated intercompany balances." Instead, these balances are netted out within the combined historical figures for assets, liabilities, revenues, and expenses across all periods shown. The capital stock and shareholders' equity sections reflect the combined and adjusted historical equity of all entities.

### 2. The $2.947 Billion Cash Paid in the xAI Merger
This is a specific transaction **between SpaceX and the shareholders of xAI**, not an intercompany balance that was eliminated.

*   **Details from the Excerpts:** "Pursuant to the terms of the xAI Merger on February 2, 2026, the Company issued, prior to the 2026 Stock Split, 321.7 million shares of Class A Common Stock, 121.7 million shares of Class B Common Stock **and paid $2,947 million in cash** to holders of xAI Common Stock and Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock."
*   **Nature of the Payment:** This cash payment was part of the **consideration paid by SpaceX to acquire xAI**. It was a payment to external shareholders of xAI (e.g., former investors, employees with stock) as part of the merger exchange. This amount would have been reflected in SpaceX's balance sheet post-merger as a reduction of cash (and a corresponding adjustment to equity, given the common control accounting).

### Summary
In short:
*   **Intercompany Eliminations:** All intercompany balances (e.g., receivables/payables for services, loans, or other transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X) from prior to the mergers were completely eliminated and are not shown as separate items in the historical financial statements.
*   **Specific Cash Payment:** The **$2,947 million cash** paid in the xAI merger was a specific consideration paid to xAI's shareholders and is a distinct transaction disclosed in the merger details.

The financial statements present a unified view of the combined entity, as if it had existed in this form throughout all prior periods, which inherently means the historical intercompany complexities have been removed from view.

steps.reasoning_content: Let me also look for any additional details about pre-merger intercompany activity, particularly any disclosures about intercompany loans or capital contributions between the entities.

arguments.prompt: In {{spaceX_s1}}, find any discussion of intercompany loans, capital contributions, or equity transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings prior to the mergers. Also look for any disclosures about how the X Holdings merger into xAI worked (March 2025) and any intercompany activity between those entities. Look for specific note references about the mergers and pre-merger relationships.

results.content:
Based on the provided excerpts from SpaceX's S-1 filing, here is a detailed analysis of the intercompany relationships and merger mechanics between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings.

### **1. Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity (Loans, Capital Contributions, Equity Transactions)**

The excerpts **do not explicitly disclose** specific intercompany loans or capital contributions between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings prior to the mergers. However, the accounting treatment and related-party disclosures provide clear evidence of a deeply intertwined financial relationship under common control.

**Key Evidence of Intercompany Financial Integration:**
*   **Common Control:** The documents repeatedly state the mergers were accounted for as "reorganizations of entities under common control" because Elon Musk had a "controlling financial interest" and "majority voting interest" in SpaceX, xAI, and X (formerly Twitter) throughout the periods presented.
*   **Retrospective Combination & Elimination:** The consolidated financial statements are presented as if the entities had always been combined. This required "retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts" and the elimination of "all historical related party transactions between the entities."
*   **Implicit Integration:** The common-control accounting itself implies that the entities were managed as part of a single economic unit prior to the formal mergers, with financial activities likely structured as internal transfers or capital allocations within that controlled group. The specific terms (e.g., interest rates on intercompany loans) are not detailed in these excerpts.

**Specific Disclosed Related-Party Transactions (Post- or Across Merger):**
While not "pre-merger" loans between the core entities, the file details significant transactions involving affiliated entities (Tesla, The Boring Company) that illustrate the ecosystem's financial links:
*   **Commercial Agreements:** xAI had significant agreements with Tesla ($506M in goods/services in 2025).
*   **Advertising:** Tesla purchased advertising on the X platform ($4M in 2025).
*   **Aircraft Usage:** SpaceX invoiced Tesla for CEO Elon Musk's use of company aircraft.
*   **Lease:** X Holdings had an office lease with The Boring Company.

### **2. Details of the X Holdings Merger into xAI (March 28, 2025)**

The documents provide clear mechanical details of this step in the consolidation:

*   **Transaction Date:** March 28, 2025.
*   **Structure:** xAI acquired X Holdings (and X.AI Corp.). X and X.AI Corp. became wholly-owned subsidiaries of xAI.
*   **Consideration/Exchange Ratio:** It was a share exchange. **Each class of X Holdings common stock was converted into 2.776 shares of xAI common stock of the same class.** Each class of X.AI Corp. common stock converted into 1.000 share of xAI common stock of the same class.
*   **Accounting:** This was the first step in the common-control reorganization. X Holdings' assets and liabilities were brought into the xAI group at their historical carrying amounts.

### **3. Intercompany Activity Between X Holdings and xAI Prior to Merger**

The excerpts **do not itemize specific intercompany loans or transactions** between X Holdings and xAI for the period before March 28, 2025. However, the context strongly indicates they operated under common control (Musk):

*   The entire period of X Holdings being owned by Musk (from October 2022) and xAI (from its inception in March 2023) is treated as part of a single reporting entity's history.
*   The requirement to eliminate "all historical related party transactions" for the retrospective presentation confirms such transactions existed but were internal to the combined group for reporting purposes.

### **4. Critical Note References for Merger Details**

The documents point to the following notes as the primary sources for merger and equity details:
*   **Note 1 - Nature of Business:** Describes the mergers and the common-control accounting treatment.
*   **Note 12 / Note 13 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders’ Equity:** This note (numbered 12 in some excerpts, 13 in others) is cited as containing the critical details on:
    *   The conversion of xAI's redeemable convertible preferred stock into SpaceX Class A & B Common Stock at the **0.1433 exchange ratio**.
    *   The full capital stock and equity adjustments resulting from the mergers.
    *   The specifics of Tesla's $2B investment in xAI, which converted into SpaceX shares post-merger.
*   **Share-Based Compensation Note:** Details the conversion of xAI and X employee stock options and RSUs into SpaceX awards using the respective exchange ratios (0.1433 for xAI, 2.776 for X).

### **Summary**

In essence, while the prospectus does not provide a ledger of specific intercompany loans, it frames SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings as components of a single economic entity under Elon Musk's control long before their legal mergers in 2025 and 2026. The financial statements are presented accordingly, combining their histories and eliminating any internal transfers. The mechanical details of the mergers are specified through precise share-exchange ratios that converted the legacy capital structures into SpaceX's capitalization table. For the granular detail on all equity and preferred stock conversions, **Note 12/13** is the definitive source.

arguments.final_result:
## Related-Party Relationships and Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity in the SpaceX Consolidated Structure

### Overview of Consolidated Structure

Following the common-control mergers — X Holdings into xAI on March 28, 2025, and xAI into SpaceX on February 2, 2026 — the consolidated entity encompasses SpaceX, xAI, X Holdings (X), and X.AI Corp. The filings treat these entities as components of a single economic unit under Elon Musk's controlling financial interest throughout all historical periods presented. All financial statements have been retrospectively recast under common-control accounting.

---

### Related-Party Relationships Within the Consolidated Structure

The filing identifies several layers of related-party relationships:

**1. Elon Musk (Founder, CEO, CTO, Chairman & Principal Shareholder)**
- Controls SpaceX, xAI, and X through majority voting interest
- Also CEO, Technoking, director, and ~20% shareholder of Tesla
- Transactions with SpaceX include: security services ($2M–$4M annually, 2023–2025), aircraft usage reimbursements, and $1,421M in stock purchases in 2025

**2. Affiliated Musk Entities**
- **Musk Industries LLC**: Lease payments from xAI for office space ($0.5M in 2024, $2M in 2025)
- **The Boring Company**: Lease payments from X ($0.1M in 2024, $1M in 2025); construction services for SpaceX ($1M in 2025)
- **Craft Aviation Services, LLC**: Aircraft maintenance for SpaceX ($1M–$3M annually, 2023–2025)

**3. Antonio J. Gracias & Valor Equity Partners**
- Gracias is a SpaceX board member and founder/CEO/CIO of Valor Equity Partners
- Three major equipment (AI infrastructure hardware) lease agreements with aggregate cash payments of $6,986M, $6,633M, and $6,587M over the lease terms
- Total lease payments: $885M (2025), $857M (Jan–Feb 2026)
- Classified as **failed sale-leaseback transactions** and recorded as debt: ~$1,121M (current) and ~$7,920M (non-current) as of March 31, 2026
- API service payments from X to Valor: $1M (2024), $1M (2025)

**4. Tesla, Inc.**
- Tesla invested **$2,000M** in xAI via Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, which converted into the right to acquire 3.8 million SpaceX Class A Common Shares (pre-split) post-merger
- SpaceX purchased Tesla Megapacks ($191M in 2024, $506M in 2025, $34M in Q1 2026) and Cybertrucks ($131M in 2025)
- Tesla purchased advertising on the X platform ($4M in 2025)
- SpaceX invoiced Tesla for Musk's aircraft usage

**5. Gwynne Shotwell & Bret Johnsen**
- President/COO and CFO who co-own a personal aircraft; occasionally used for SpaceX business subject to reimbursement

---

### Where the Filing Discusses Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity

**1. Note 1 — Nature of Business / Business Combinations**
- Describes the X Holdings merger into xAI (March 28, 2025): X Holdings shares converted at a 2.776:1 ratio into xAI shares; X and X.AI Corp. became wholly-owned subsidiaries of xAI
- Describes the xAI merger into SpaceX (February 2, 2026): SpaceX issued 321.7M Class A shares, 121.7M Class B shares, and $2,947M in cash to xAI stockholders
- Explains common-control accounting: net assets combined at historical carrying amounts with no goodwill recognized
- States: "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation"

**2. Note 2 — Summary of Significant Accounting Policies**
- Reaffirms the broad principle: "All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation"
- Notes this constitutes a "change in reporting entity" with retrospective restatement of all periods

**3. "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" Section**
- This is the primary location for the **detailed enumeration** of specific pre-merger intercompany activity
- Itemizes all transactions between the consolidated entities and their affiliates, including commercial agreements, leases, services, and asset usage
- Reports dollar amounts by year (2023, 2024, 2025, and Jan–Feb 2026) for each category
- Threshold disclosure: transactions exceeding $120,000 since January 1, 2023
- Scope includes all transactions involving directors, executive officers, >5% stockholders, and their affiliates

**4. Note 12/13 — Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock & Shareholders' Equity**
- Details the conversion mechanics of xAI redeemable convertible preferred stock into SpaceX shares at a 0.1433 exchange ratio
- Documents Tesla's $2B xAI investment and its post-merger conversion into SpaceX equity
- Covers the full capital stock and equity adjustments from the mergers

**5. Share-Based Compensation Note**
- Details conversion of xAI and X employee stock options and RSUs into SpaceX awards using the exchange ratios (0.1433 for xAI, 2.776 for X)

---

### Key Accounting Implications

- **No separate entity financials provided**: Because of the retrospective common-control presentation, standalone financial statements for xAI and X are not available
- **Eliminated intercompany balances**: The filing does not provide a line-by-line breakdown of specific intercompany receivables/payables that were eliminated; they are netted within the combined historical figures
- **Implicit pre-merger integration**: The common-control framework implies the entities operated as part of a single economic unit prior to formal mergers, with financial activities structured as internal transfers within the Musk-controlled group

---

### Sources

| Source | URL |
|--------|-----|
| SpaceX S-1 Filing (May 20, 2026) | https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm |
